title: 📊 Engineering Overview [v2]
description: High-level summary of all DORA metrics and Copilot adoption/impact indicators for the last 28 days.

panels.description: Data mode banner — shows whether this dashboard displays live GitHub data (green), synthetic seed data (amber), or demo data (blue).

targets.rawSql:
SELECT
  CASE mode
    WHEN 'seed' THEN '🌱 Synthetic Seed Data — ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
    ELSE '📡 ' || COALESCE(source_label, '')
  END AS "Data Source"
FROM data_mode
ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1

options.content:
# 📊 Engineering Overview

**All Four DORA Pillars — At a Glance**

## What it Shows
This overview dashboard presents the four core DORA (DevOps Research & Assessment) metrics side-by-side, giving engineering leaders a single-pane view of team health across throughput and stability.

| Metric | Pillar | What it Tracks |
|---|---|---|
| **Deployment Frequency** | Throughput | How often code ships to production |
| **Lead Time for Changes** | Throughput | Time from commit to production |
| **Change Failure Rate** | Stability | % of deployments causing incidents |
| **MTTR** | Stability | Time to restore service after failure |

## Why it Matters
DORA benchmarks are validated predictors of software delivery performance and organisational outcomes. High performers score well on *all four* metrics simultaneously — throughput gains must not come at the cost of stability.

## How to Use This Dashboard
- Use the stat panels to get an instant RAG (red/amber/green) status for each pillar
- Drill into any pillar's dedicated dashboard for trend charts and actionable detail
- Compare cohort breakdowns on the **DORA × Copilot** dashboard to assess Copilot's impact

panels.description: Engineering Overview — all four DORA pillars at a glance with drill-down links.

panels.title: Deployment Frequency
panels.description:
### What it measures
Average number of successful deployments per week over the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Higher frequency indicates smaller batch sizes, more automation, and faster value delivery. Elite performers deploy multiple times per day.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Only counts deployments with a 'success' status
- Averaged over a fixed 28-day window

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id)::float / 4 AS "Deployments/Week"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Lead Time (median hours)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Median time from PR merge to successful deployment over the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Shorter lead times mean faster feedback loops and value delivery. Elite teams achieve less than 1 hour.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Only considers PRs linked to successful deployments
- PRs without a merged_at timestamp are excluded

targets.rawSql:
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (d.created_at - pr.merged_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "Lead Time (hours)"
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.number = dpr.pr_number
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND pr.merged_at IS NOT NULL
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Change Fail Rate
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of deployments that resulted in a production incident within 24 hours over the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Lower CFR reflects higher code quality and better testing practices. Elite teams maintain below 5%.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Incidents are identified by the 'incident' label on GitHub Issues
- Only considers deployments in the 28-day window

targets.rawSql:
WITH deploy_failures AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT d.deployment_id
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  JOIN issues i ON i.labels::jsonb @> '[{"name":"incident"}]'
    AND i.created_at BETWEEN d.created_at AND d.created_at + INTERVAL '24 hours'
  WHERE ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT df.deployment_id)::float /
  NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT d.deployment_id), 0) * 100 AS "Change Fail Rate (%)"
FROM deployments d
LEFT JOIN deploy_failures df ON df.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
WHERE ds.state = 'success'
AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: MTTR (median)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Median time from deployment failure to the next successful deployment in the same environment, over the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Lower MTTR indicates faster incident response and recovery capabilities. Elite teams recover in less than 1 hour.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Recovery is approximated as the next successful deployment after a failure
- Requires at least one failure and one subsequent success to compute

targets.rawSql:
WITH failure_windows AS (
  SELECT
    d.deployment_id,
    d.created_at AS failed_at,
    LEAD(d.created_at) OVER (PARTITION BY d.environment ORDER BY d.created_at) AS recovered_at
  FROM deployments d
  JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.deployment_id
  WHERE ds.state = 'failure'
  AND d.created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (
  ORDER BY EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (recovered_at - failed_at)) / 3600.0
) AS "MTTR (hours)"
FROM failure_windows
WHERE recovered_at IS NOT NULL

panels.title: Active Copilot Seats
panels.description:
### What it measures
Number of Copilot seats with recorded activity in the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Shows real adoption versus provisioned seats. A large gap between total and active seats indicates underutilization.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Activity is based on the last_activity_at field from the copilot_seats table
- Does not differentiate between editor types

targets.rawSql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS "Active Seats" FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Acceptance Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Ratio of Copilot-suggested lines that were accepted (added) versus total lines suggested, over the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Higher acceptance rate indicates Copilot suggestions are relevant and useful to developers.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Based on aggregated org-level metrics from copilot_org_metrics
- A low rate may reflect personal preference, not suggestion quality

targets.rawSql:
SELECT COALESCE(ROUND(SUM(loc_added_sum)::numeric / NULLIF(SUM(loc_suggested_to_add_sum), 0) * 100, 1), 0) AS "Acceptance Rate (%)"
FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28

panels.title: Copilot PR Rate (%)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Percentage of merged PRs authored by developers with an active Copilot seat in the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Indicates how much of the team's output involves Copilot-assisted development. Growing rate suggests increasing adoption impact.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- A user may have a Copilot seat but not use it for every PR
- Only counts merged PRs

targets.rawSql:
WITH copilot_users AS (
  SELECT assignee_id FROM copilot_seats WHERE last_activity_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'
)
SELECT ROUND(
  COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN cu.assignee_id IS NOT NULL THEN pr.id END)::numeric * 100.0
  / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT pr.id), 0), 1
) AS "Copilot PR Rate (%)"
FROM pull_requests pr
LEFT JOIN copilot_users cu ON pr.user_id = cu.assignee_id
WHERE pr.merged_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '28 days'

panels.title: Lines Accepted (28d)
panels.description:
### What it measures
Total number of Copilot-suggested lines accepted by developers in the last 28 days.

### What insight it offers
Absolute volume of Copilot-assisted code production. Useful for tracking growth in AI-assisted output over time.

### ⚠️ Caveats
- Derived from aggregated org-level daily metrics
- High volume does not guarantee high quality of accepted code

targets.rawSql: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(loc_added_sum), 0) AS "Lines Accepted (28d)" FROM copilot_org_metrics WHERE day >= NOW()::date - 28
